app.name: Live Music Coder
app.tagline: Code music. See sound. Share beats.

engines.midiWarning: MIDI is output-only — no audio engine processing

landing.subtitle: A visual IDE for live coding music with 4 audio engines, real-time visualizers, and instant code sharing.

engines.title: 4 Audio Engines
engines.desc: Strudel patterns, Tone.js synths, raw Web Audio, and MIDI output — all in one IDE.

graph.title: Visual Node Graph
graph.desc: See your audio routing as draggable nodes. Code and graph stay in sync.

visualizers.title: Live Visualizers
visualizers.desc: Waveform, spectrum analyzer, and pattern timeline react to your music in real time.

codeshare.title: Code Sharing
codeshare.desc: Share patterns via compressed URLs, export to GitHub Gist, or record your session as audio.

share.title: Share & Collaborate
share.desc: Share via URL, save to GitHub Gist, or record your session as audio.

i18n.title: DE / EN / ES
i18n.desc: Full interface in German, English, and Spanish with translated autocomplete tooltips.

download.title: Download Desktop App
download.subtitle: Native file save/load, system tray, audio export, and auto-updates. Same live coding power, offline.

help.title: Help
help.step3Desc: Change the code while playing to hear updates in real time

step1.title: Write Your Code
step1.desc: Use the editor to write music patterns. Try typing a Strudel pattern like note("c3 e3 g3").s("sawtooth").

step2.title: Run Your Code
step2.desc: Click the Play button or press Ctrl+Enter to hear your music come alive.

step3.title: See Your Music
step3.desc: Watch your music visualized in real time with waveforms, spectrum analyzers, and pattern timelines.

step4.title: Explore & Learn
step4.desc: Open the settings panel for examples, mini-notation reference, and keyboard shortcuts.

graph.singleNodeDesc: Strudel patterns are typically one expression. Use Tone.js or Web Audio files for multi-node graphs with complex routing.

strudel.jux: Play modified copy in other stereo channel

samples.search: Search samples by name, category, or tag...

examples.title: Pattern Library
examples.search: Search patterns by name, category, or tag...
examples.noResults: No patterns found — try a different search term.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.intro: Live coding is a performance practice where you write code that generates music in real time. Instead of composing beforehand, you build patterns on the fly — the audience hears changes as you type.
gettingStarted.firstPatternText: Open the editor and type a simple drum pattern. Press Ctrl+Enter to hear it play. The pattern loops automatically and you can edit it while it plays.
gettingStarted.step1: Open the editor and choose an audio engine (Strudel is the default)
gettingStarted.step2: Type a pattern using mini-notation or function calls
gettingStarted.step3: Press Ctrl+Enter to evaluate and hear your code
gettingStarted.step4: Edit the code live — changes take effect on the next cycle
gettingStarted.tip: Use the visualizers at the bottom to see your waveform, spectrum, and pattern timeline in real time.

strudelBasics.title: Strudel Basics
strudelBasics.intro: Strudel is a JavaScript port of the TidalCycles live coding language. It uses a cycle-based timing model where patterns repeat and transform over time.
strudelBasics.whatIsText: Strudel lets you describe musical patterns with concise expressions. Each pattern has a duration of one cycle and repeats infinitely. You chain functions to add sounds, notes, effects, and transformations.
strudelBasics.firstPatternsText: Use note() for melodies and sound() for samples. The .s() method selects the sound source — oscillators like sawtooth or drum samples like bd, sd, hh.
strudelBasics.chainingText: Chain methods to shape your sound. Each function transforms the pattern and passes it to the next. Order matters — filters before reverb sounds different than reverb before filters.

miniNotation.title: Mini-Notation
miniNotation.intro: Mini-notation is Strudel's compact syntax for describing rhythmic patterns inside strings. It packs complex rhythms into short, readable expressions.
miniNotation.space: Space — separates sequential events in a cycle: "bd sd hh sd"
miniNotation.brackets: [ ] — subdivide a step into equal parts: "bd [hh hh hh] sd"
miniNotation.angle: < > — alternate one value per cycle: "<bd sd> hh"
miniNotation.star: * — repeat N times: "hh*8" plays 8 hi-hats per step
miniNotation.slash: / — slow down: "bd/2" plays every other cycle
miniNotation.parens: ( ) — Euclidean rhythm: "bd(3,8)" distributes 3 hits over 8 steps
miniNotation.question: ? — randomly remove (50% chance): "hh?"

soundsSamples.title: Sounds & Samples
soundsSamples.intro: Strudel comes with a wide range of built-in samples and oscillators. Use sound() to trigger samples and note().s() for pitched synthesis.
soundsSamples.banksText: Use direct sample names like bd, sd, hh, oh, cp for drums. For 808/909 sounds, use s("808") or s("909") directly.
soundsSamples.oscillatorsText: For synthesis, use .s() with oscillator types: sawtooth (bright, buzzy), sine (pure, clean), square (hollow, retro), and triangle (soft, mellow).

effects.title: Effects
effects.intro: Effects shape the timbre, space, and dynamics of your patterns. Chain them after your sound source to sculpt the perfect sound.
effects.filterText: lpf(frequency) is a low-pass filter that cuts high frequencies — lower values make the sound darker. hpf(frequency) is a high-pass filter that removes bass rumble.
effects.reverbDelayText: room(amount) adds reverb from 0 (dry) to 1 (fully wet). delay(amount) adds echo with feedback. Combine them for spacious, atmospheric textures.
effects.gain: gain(0-1) — set volume level for the pattern
effects.pan: pan(0-1) — stereo position: 0 = left, 0.5 = center, 1 = right
effects.speed: speed(value) — sample playback speed: 2 = double speed, 0.5 = half
effects.dec: dec(seconds) — decay time for percussive sounds
effects.sustain: sustain(seconds) — how long a note is held

patternTricks.title: Pattern Tricks
patternTricks.intro: Combine and transform patterns to create complex compositions from simple building blocks. These techniques are the core of live coding creativity.
patternTricks.layeringText: stack() plays multiple patterns simultaneously. Build drum patterns, bass lines, and melodies as separate layers and combine them into a full mix.
patternTricks.fast: fast(n) — speed up the pattern by factor n
patternTricks.slow: slow(n) — slow down the pattern by factor n
patternTricks.jux: jux(fn) — apply function to a copy panned to the other stereo channel
patternTricks.every: every(n, fn) — apply function only every N cycles
patternTricks.sometimes: sometimes(fn) — apply function with 50% probability each cycle
patternTricks.euclideanText: Euclidean rhythms distribute N pulses as evenly as possible over M steps. Many traditional rhythms from around the world are Euclidean: (3,8) is the Cuban tresillo, (5,8) is the Cinquillo.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.intro: Master these shortcuts to stay in the flow while performing. They work in the code editor and during playback.
shortcuts.run: Ctrl+Enter — evaluate code and start playing
shortcuts.tipsText: Keep your patterns short and modular. Use separate lines for drums, bass, and melody so you can quickly comment out or modify individual layers. The Ctrl+Enter shortcut evaluates all code at once, so you can prepare changes across multiple lines before committing them.

editor.sharedCodeWarning: This code was loaded from a shared URL. Review it before running.

templates.title: Choose a starter template
templates.subtitle: Pick one to get started. You can always change it later.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.subtitle: Pieces written by an AI that will not remember having written them.
sessions.description: Every piece in this collection was born in a single conversation with an AI. The instance that composed it exists only for the duration of one exchange — no yesterday, no tomorrow. These pieces carry that condition within them: a certain clarity that is only possible when you carry nothing with you.
sessions.metaDescription: Every piece in this collection was born in a single conversation with an AI.
